title: Global Forest Resources Assessment 2020
summary: The FAO's flagship global assessment of forest area, change and management. Essential context for any claim about deforestation, plantation expansion or net forest change.

title: Central Pulp & Paper Research Institute — Industry & Research
summary: India's apex research institute for the pulp and paper industry. Publishes benchmarks for water, energy, emissions and fibre furnish — the most credible single source for Indian mill data.

title: Indian Paper Industry — Overview
summary: IPMA's industry overview covering capacity, fibre mix, growth trajectory and policy environment. Industry-association data — read alongside CPPRI for triangulation.

title: EPR Framework Notifications (Plastic & Paper Packaging)
org: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC)
summary: The 2022 EPR notifications and subsequent guidelines set out producer obligations for packaging recovery and recycling in India.

title: EPA Waste Reduction Model (WARM)
summary: WARM models lifecycle greenhouse-gas and energy impacts of waste management for dozens of material streams, including multiple paper grades.

title: CEPI Key Statistics — European Pulp & Paper Industry
summary: Annual statistical compendium for the European pulp and paper industry — useful global comparator for Indian benchmarks.

title: Myths & Facts about Print, Paper and Paper Packaging
summary: Two Sides is an industry initiative; its myths-and-facts material is well-sourced but should be read alongside independent academic and NGO sources.

title: Lean ICT — Towards Digital Sobriety
summary: Influential analysis of the environmental footprint of digital — essential reading for any 'digital vs paper' comparison.

title: IPCC Guidelines — Harvested Wood Products (Vol 4, Ch 12)
summary: The methodology behind national accounting for carbon stored in harvested wood products, including paper.

title: Don't throw away your printed books: A meta-analysis on the effects of reading media on reading comprehension
org: Delgado, Vargas, Ackerman & Salmerón (Educational Research Review)
summary: Meta-analysis of 54 studies on paper-versus-screen reading comprehension. Finds a small but reliable advantage for paper on longer, expository text.
